statement.en: Uniqueness Ratio as a Predictor of a Privacy Leakage
statement.summary: This paper introduces Uniqueness Ratio as a pre-join metric for predicting privacy leakage in integrated databases. It analyzes how attribute combinations become identifying only after dataset joins, enabling early risk assessment before data integration using machine learning.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper introduces a novel metric (Uniqueness Ratio) for privacy risk prediction in database integration. Status is provisional: metric definition appears sound in principle, but validation against real-world datasets and comparison to established privacy metrics (k-anonymity, differential privacy bounds) not yet visible in abstract. The predictive claim requires empirical confirmation post-publication.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 結合後の一意性の爆発が、隠れていた識別子を暴露する。
